## The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences appoints new general manager 28 July 2025

Hospitality veteran, Edward Bracken joins the Mayfair-based property

Share This Article

The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences is delighted to announce the appointment of its new General Manager, Edward Bracken marking a new era for the award-winning hotel.With over 20 years of hospitality experience, Edward brings a wealth of knowledge and a proven track record of delivering guest-centric results. His vision is to further enhance the hotel’s reputation by fostering an empowered team and offering exceptional, personalised guest experiences.Originally from Limerick, Edward studied at the Shannon College of Hotel Management and has since built an impressive career across a number of renowned London Hotels including The Dixon, Tower Bridge, Lost Property St. Paul’s London, Marriott Kensington The Gore; and the five-star Pelham Hotel.In addition, Edward remains closely connected to his Irish roots, serving as a trustee and director of The London Irish Centre.

Commenting on his appointment, Edward said, “I’m truly excited to be joining The Athenaeum Hotel and Residences. This is a hotel with deep character and history, and I look forward to working closely with the team to enhance the guest journey and create new stories for this celebrated Mayfair hotel”.

Alistair Darby, Chief Executive of parent company Sir Richard Sutton Ltd said, “Edward has had a successful career nurturing high performing teams. With ambitious development plans on the horizon, we’re thrilled to welcome Edward to the team as we invest in The Athenaeum to maintain its status as one of the finest five-star hotels in Mayfair”.

The Athenaeum Hotel and Residences is an award-winning, five-star hotel offering stunning views over Green Park in the heart of Mayfair. Embodying proud British heritage with a nod to its Art Deco roots, the hotel offers beautifully designed rooms, suites and private residences – each celebrating British craftsmanship and timeless style.

# Your stay at The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences

The Athenaeum Hotel & Residencies is a five-star gem in Mayfair, rooted in London culture. Business, leisure, or simple pleasure, enjoy delicious dining, crafted cocktails and a restful night’s sleep.

09/04/25

#### 1. Where can I eat?

116 at The Athenaeum: Our stylish restaurant serves up fresh, seasonal British cuisine. Sourced as locally as possible you can enjoy breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Try our standout dry-aged steaks or fresh Cornish fish.

Afternoon Tea: Experience a British tradition. Our Fancies & Foliage afternoon tea features an array of delicate finger sandwiches, homemade scones, and exquisite pastries. Upgrade your afternoon and add our blending experience. Guided through over 45 bespoke flavours you'll create your own brew from our loose leaf teas.

In-Room Dining: Late night craving? Or hibernation mode on? In-room dining is available 24 hours a day. Be it a hearty breakfast or a afternoon snack, the menu offers plenty of options.

Discover our dining options

#### 2. What are the rooms and suites like?

There are plenty of options, all designed for supreme comfort and style:

Luxury Bedrooms: Featuring the comfiest king-size beds, floor-to-ceiling windows, and modern furnishings, our rooms provide a peaceful refuge in the heart of London.

Suites: If you need a bit more space, our suites include separate living areas, and stunning views over Green Park or the city skyline.

Residences: Perfect for families or extended stays, the residences offer private apartment-style living, complete with kitchens and dining spaces.

You can view more information on our accommodation here!

#### 3. What about the bar?

Head to The Bar at The Athenaeum. Our chic and intimate space serves handcrafted cocktails, fine wines, and a carefully curated selection of spirits. In the mood for a signature cocktail or a classic G&T? Our bartenders will ensure a perfect pour.

Or take a seat on our outdoor terrace. Overlook Green Park while you sip a cocktail or two.

#### 4. What wellness facilities does the hotel offer?

The spa offers an array of treatments, including deep-tissue massages, hot stone therapy, rejuvenating facials, and body wraps designed to help guests relax and refresh. Using organic, premium skincare brand Temple Spa, all the details are thought of. With a sauna, steam room and hot-tub, it's perfect for detoxifying and unwinding after a long day exploring London.

Fancy a workout? Stay active and visit our fully equipped gym is open 24/7, featuring cutting-edge cardio machines, free weights, and strength training equipment.

Explore the Spa at The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences.

#### 5. Is the hotel family-friendly?

Absolutely. It's an excellent choice for families, our residences include fully equipped kitchens, separate living areas, and bunk beds on request. Perfect for those traveling with children. Our dedicated kids' concierge, can arrange museum visits, family-friendly theatre tickets, and bespoke in-room entertainment.

Younger guests are treated to special welcome packs, kid-friendly dining options, and even milk and cookies before bedtime. With Green Park just across the street, there’s plenty of space for outdoor play and family strolls.

#### 6. Can I host private events or meetings?

We have several spacious meeting and event spaces. All with natural light, state-of-the-art tech and bang in the middle of London. A corporate dinner, a wedding reception or an all-team meeting. Our team will work with you to create your event.

#### 7. Are there any special dining experiences available?

Of course. Seasonal dining experiences, tasting menus curated by Chef, wine-pairings and cocktail masterclasses. We have a lot.

If you're looking for a more intimate dining experience, private dining options are available, offering bespoke menus tailored to your’ preferences. The hotel also hosts exclusive culinary events, including guest chef appearances and seasonal feasts.

Get in touch with any further questions here

## Welcome to The Athenaeum

A Mayfair address with a story

Right opposite Green Park. Steps from Buckingham Palace. Where your London stays feel like coming home.

Since 1850, this Piccadilly corner has been part club, part residence, now it’s yours. You’ll stay where celebrities once edited films, where walls turned from club to residence, and the park is just across the road. Here, you’ll find comfort, calm, and a touch of London magic.

### Book direct. Enjoy more

Looking to get more from your stay? Book direct and enjoy an extra 12% off our lowest prices, plus get £35 to spend in our bar or restaurant each night you stay.

Book Now

### Have a night on us

Make the most of your time in London with an extra night on us. Stay four nights and pay for three, with breakfast included each morning. Arrive, drop your bags with Daniel or Miguel, and let Mayfair unfold at your own pace.

Book with promo code STAYLONGER.

Book Now

1 2

## Rooms and Residences

A true London home should feel both refined and relaxed. Our rooms, suites and townhouse residences offer space to breathe in the heart of Mayfair, blending Art Deco elegance with contemporary London design. Many overlook Green Park, and all provide the kind of generous space that’s rare in this part of the city.

- Family-friendly and dog-friendly options
- Eighteen private townhouse residences
- Suites with separate living areas
- Spacious, thoughtfully appointed rooms

PICK YOUR ROOM

## Dining and drinks

Modern British flavour served with Mayfair ease. At 116 at The Athenaeum, seasonal dishes are refined, generous and rooted in London’s vibrant food scene — the ideal place to refuel and reset during your day. The Terrace offers relaxed open-air dining beside Piccadilly, while our Bar provides an intimate escape for coffee, cocktails and easy conversation.

- The Bar: warm, inviting and unmistakably Mayfair
- The Terrace: relaxed outdoor dining and drinks
- Afternoon Tea with a modern twist
- 116 at The Athenaeum: contemporary British dining

BOOK YOUR TABLE

## Meetings and Events

Polished, private and perfectly placed. Our meeting and event spaces blend Mayfair sophistication with the ease of a private townhouse. State-of-the-art technology ensures seamless in-person or hybrid sessions, while thoughtful touches like great coffee or a restorative lunch, keep everyone refreshed. Every detail is looked after with care, because making guests feel special is what we do best.

- Dedicated planners to ensure everything runs beautifully
- Tailored menus from our culinary team
- Advanced audio-visual and hybrid connectivity
- Natural daylight and park views in select spaces
- Elegant rooms for meetings, dinners and private events

PLAN YOUR EVENT

## SPA AND FITNESS

In a city that never pauses, The Athenaeum invites you to exhale. Our spa offers restorative treatments, a steam room and quiet spaces designed for unhurried wellbeing, using natural British products delivered with thoughtful care. For those who like to keep moving, our 24-hour gym with Peloton and Technogym equipment complements any routine.

- In-room treatments on request
- Steam room and relaxation area
- Massages and facials with natural ingredients

PLAN YOUR VISIT

## In the heart of London life

Step outside The Athenaeum and London’s most iconic parks, cultural landmarks and shopping streets unfold just moments from your door.

Open spaces

With Green Park directly opposite and Hyde Park only a few steps away, peaceful morning walks and wide open lawns become part of daily life during your stay. Families can collect kites or bikes from our Concierge for an afternoon in the park, while four legged guests have the perfect setting for a gentle stroll. Buckingham Palace is within a very short walking distance, just beyond the trees of Green Park.

Explore more

Shopping

Our Mayfair address places you beside some of London’s finest shopping. Luxury boutiques on Bond Street, the timeless elegance of Burlington Arcade and the historic charm of Fortnum and Mason are moments away. Oxford Street, Regent Street, Selfridges, Liberty of London, Harrods and Hamleys are close enough for effortless browsing, while the independent spirit of Shepherd Market offers a charming contrast just around the corner.

Explore more

Culture

The Royal Academy of Arts is moments from the hotel and BAFTA sits along Piccadilly, placing exhibitions, screenings and talks on your doorstep. Curzon Mayfair offers classic and contemporary cinema in a beautiful Grade II listed setting, while Wigmore Hall presents world class chamber music just a short walk away. London’s theatres and galleries surround the neighbourhood, giving you easy access to the best of the capital’s art, film and performance scene.

Explore more

1 3

## Discover our history

The word ‘Athenaeum’ loosely translates as ‘library’ and is derived from the Greek name Athena, the goddess of wisdom. Our fascinating history has been shaped by culture, literature and the arts.

### 116 Piccadilly

We’ve got an enviable address in the heart of Mayfair and we’re proud of that. Surrounded by big names like the Ritz and Fortnum and Mason, The Athenaeum Hotel has long been an independent refuge in a sea of brands. A place where we deliver extraordinary service with confidence and discretion and we acknowledge each guest as our long lost friend. Not formulaic, totally personal.

Our roots are firmly grounded in culture, arts and creativity, everything that makes Mayfair so special. That story started in 1850 when Henry Hope chose our building as his home.

Henry Hope was an MP between 1833 and 1852 and his passion was in the arts. He was the first resident of 116 Piccadilly and his extravagant interiors and lavish displays of art caught the eye of Charles Dickens.

Our connection to the arts began with Hope House which held one of Europe’s finest art collections. When Henry died aged just 45, his wife sold Hope House to the fashionable Junior Athenaeum Club. Marking a new era.

## From an art house to artists

The dawn of a new era

Gentlemen’s clubs were all the rage in the Victorian era, and the Junior Athenaeum surged in popularity as it entertained the elite of London society. The club was particularly favoured by gentlemen connected with literature, science and art.

In the 1930s, the building was transformed into an Art Deco apartment block.

Four decades later, The Athenaeum was snapped up by British entertainment group The Rank Organisation. The company renovated the apartments into an iconic hotel to accommodate its movie stars whenever they were filming at London’s famous Pinewood and Ealing Studios and here started a new legacy.

The Athenaeum Hotel became the place where celebrities could retreat from the public eye. The quirky back passage to the residences was often used to escape the glare of the paparazzi. The team were not unfamiliar with handling big stars with the upmost discretion.

Big moments in history happened right here. Take That announced their split from a suite. Stephen Spielberg worked on ET during his stay with us.

Today, we continue our passion with the arts, local culture and service with discretion. We champion local artists, we celebrate our local Mayfair community, all sprinkled with a personal service delivered with confidence and charm.

# The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences appoints new General Manager

Hospitality veteran, Edward Bracken joins the Mayfair-based property.

14/07/25

The Athenaeum Hotel & Residences is delighted to announce the appointment of its new General Manager, Edward Bracken marking a new era for the award-winning hotel.

With over 20 years of hospitality experience, Edward brings a wealth of knowledge and a proven track record of delivering guest-centric results. His vision is to further enhance the hotel’s reputation by fostering an empowered team and offering exceptional, personalised guest experiences. Originally from Limerick, Edward studied at the Shannon College of Hotel Management and has since built an impressive career across a number of renowned London Hotels including The Dixon, Tower Bridge, Lost Property St. Paul’s London, Marriott Kensington The Gore; and the five-star Pelham Hotel. In addition, Edward remains closely connected to his Irish roots, serving as a trustee and director of The London Irish Centre.

Commenting on his appointment, Edward said, “I’m truly excited to be joining The Athenaeum Hotel and Residences. This is a hotel with deep character and history, and I look forward to working closely with the team to enhance the guest journey and create new stories for this celebrated Mayfair hotel”.Alistair Darby, Chief Executive of parent company Sir Richard Sutton Ltd said, “Edward has had a successful career nurturing high performing teams. With ambitious development plans on the horizon, we’re thrilled to welcome Edward to the team as we invest in The Athenaeum to maintain its status as one of the finest five-star hotels in Mayfair”. The Athenaeum Hotel and Residences is an award-winning, five-star hotel offering stunning views over Green Park in the heart of Mayfair. Embodying proud British heritage with a nod to its Art Deco roots, the hotel offers beautifully designed rooms, suites and private residences – each celebrating British craftsmanship and timeless style.

## Discover our history

The word ‘Athenaeum’ loosely translates as ‘library’ and is derived from the Greek name Athena, the goddess of wisdom. Our fascinating history has been shaped by culture, literature and the arts.

### 116 Piccadilly

We’ve got an enviable address in the heart of Mayfair and we’re proud of that. Surrounded by big names like the Ritz and Fortnum and Mason, The Athenaeum Hotel has long been an independent refuge in a sea of brands. A place where we deliver extraordinary service with confidence and discretion and we acknowledge each guest as our long lost friend. Not formulaic, totally personal.

Our roots are firmly grounded in culture, arts and creativity, everything that makes Mayfair so special. That story started in 1850 when Henry Hope chose our building as his home.

Henry Hope was an MP between 1833 and 1852 and his passion was in the arts. He was the first resident of 116 Piccadilly and his extravagant interiors and lavish displays of art caught the eye of Charles Dickens.

Our connection to the arts began with Hope House which held one of Europe’s finest art collections. When Henry died aged just 45, his wife sold Hope House to the fashionable Junior Athenaeum Club. Marking a new era.

## From an art house to artists

The dawn of a new era

Gentlemen’s clubs were all the rage in the Victorian era, and the Junior Athenaeum surged in popularity as it entertained the elite of London society. The club was particularly favoured by gentlemen connected with literature, science and art.

In the 1930s, the building was transformed into an Art Deco apartment block.

Four decades later, The Athenaeum was snapped up by British entertainment group The Rank Organisation. The company renovated the apartments into an iconic hotel to accommodate its movie stars whenever they were filming at London’s famous Pinewood and Ealing Studios and here started a new legacy.

The Athenaeum Hotel became the place where celebrities could retreat from the public eye. The quirky back passage to the residences was often used to escape the glare of the paparazzi. The team were not unfamiliar with handling big stars with the upmost discretion.

Big moments in history happened right here. Take That announced their split from a suite. Stephen Spielberg worked on ET during his stay with us.

Today, we continue our passion with the arts, local culture and service with discretion. We champion local artists, we celebrate our local Mayfair community, all sprinkled with a personal service delivered with confidence and charm.

## Welcome to The Athenaeum

A Mayfair address with a story

Right opposite Green Park. Steps from Buckingham Palace. Where your London stays feel like coming home.

Since 1850, this Piccadilly corner has been part club, part residence, now it’s yours. You’ll stay where celebrities once edited films, where walls turned from club to residence, and the park is just across the road. Here, you’ll find comfort, calm, and a touch of London magic.

### Spring sale

Reset, refresh, refocus.

Save up to 25% off a two-night spring escape in London.

Book Now

### Book direct. Enjoy more

Looking to get more from your stay? Book direct and enjoy an extra 12% off our lowest prices, plus get £35 to spend in our bar or restaurant each night you stay.

Book Now

### Have a night on us

Make the most of your time in London with an extra night on us. Stay four nights and pay for three, with breakfast included each morning. Arrive, drop your bags with Daniel or Miguel, and let Mayfair unfold at your own pace.

Book with promo code STAYLONGER.

Book Now

1 3

## Rooms and Residences

A true London home should feel both refined and relaxed. Our rooms, suites and townhouse residences offer space to breathe in the heart of Mayfair, blending Art Deco elegance with contemporary London design. Many overlook Green Park, and all provide the kind of generous space that’s rare in this part of the city.

- Spacious, thoughtfully appointed rooms
- Suites with separate living areas
- Eighteen private townhouse residences
- Family-friendly and dog-friendly options

PICK YOUR ROOM

## Dining and drinks

Modern British flavour served with Mayfair ease. At 116 at The Athenaeum, seasonal dishes are refined, generous and rooted in London’s vibrant food scene — the ideal place to refuel and reset during your day. The Terrace offers relaxed open-air dining beside Piccadilly, while our Bar provides an intimate escape for coffee, cocktails and easy conversation.

- 116 at The Athenaeum: contemporary British dining
- Afternoon Tea with a modern twist
- The Terrace: relaxed outdoor dining and drinks
- The Bar: warm, inviting and unmistakably Mayfair

BOOK YOUR TABLE

## Meetings and Events

Polished, private and perfectly placed. Our meeting and event spaces blend Mayfair sophistication with the ease of a private townhouse. State-of-the-art technology ensures seamless in-person or hybrid sessions, while thoughtful touches like great coffee or a restorative lunch, keep everyone refreshed. Every detail is looked after with care, because making guests feel special is what we do best.

- Elegant rooms for meetings, dinners and private events
- Natural daylight and park views in select spaces
- Advanced audio-visual and hybrid connectivity
- Tailored menus from our culinary team
- Dedicated planners to ensure everything runs beautifully

PLAN YOUR EVENT

## SPA AND FITNESS

In a city that never pauses, The Athenaeum invites you to exhale. Our spa offers restorative treatments, a steam room and quiet spaces designed for unhurried wellbeing, using natural British products delivered with thoughtful care. For those who like to keep moving, our 24-hour gym with Peloton and Technogym equipment complements any routine.

- Massages and facials with natural ingredients
- Steam room and relaxation area
- In-room treatments on request

PLAN YOUR VISIT

## In the heart of London life

Step outside The Athenaeum and London’s most iconic parks, cultural landmarks and shopping streets unfold just moments from your door.

Open spaces

With Green Park directly opposite and Hyde Park only a few steps away, peaceful morning walks and wide open lawns become part of daily life during your stay. Families can collect kites or bikes from our Concierge for an afternoon in the park, while four legged guests have the perfect setting for a gentle stroll. Buckingham Palace is within a very short walking distance, just beyond the trees of Green Park.

Explore more

Shopping

Our Mayfair address places you beside some of London’s finest shopping. Luxury boutiques on Bond Street, the timeless elegance of Burlington Arcade and the historic charm of Fortnum and Mason are moments away. Oxford Street, Regent Street, Selfridges, Liberty of London, Harrods and Hamleys are close enough for effortless browsing, while the independent spirit of Shepherd Market offers a charming contrast just around the corner.

Explore more

Culture

The Royal Academy of Arts is moments from the hotel and BAFTA sits along Piccadilly, placing exhibitions, screenings and talks on your doorstep. Curzon Mayfair offers classic and contemporary cinema in a beautiful Grade II listed setting, while Wigmore Hall presents world class chamber music just a short walk away. London’s theatres and galleries surround the neighbourhood, giving you easy access to the best of the capital’s art, film and performance scene.

Explore more
